title: Convertible 3 in 1 Toy Chest
summary: Keep your little one's room organized and clean with this multi-purpose toy chest.
description: Keep your little one's room organized and clean with this multi-purpose toy chest. With a contemporary design, this is the perfect addition to a kid's room. Learn how to build this space-saving Multifunctional toy box that converts from bench seating into a desk.

cutList.part: Back and front of the Toy Box
Lid Back Board of Toy Box (non-movable)

steps.title: Assemble the box
steps.body: Cut the sides of the toy box to size. Using a drill and a router notch out the handles on the two side boards. Using a jigsaw cut the lip into the 2 sides and front panel boards. Now assemble using pocket screws the base of the toy box.

steps.title: Attach the lid
steps.body:
Attach the lid to the top of the toy box. Start by attaching the back fixed portion of the lid. This is where the backrest board will rest when the toy box is a bench.
Now using the narrow hinges attach the lid in front and flush with the fixed board. I recommend using a router to recess the hinge.

steps.title: Back Rest
steps.body: Cut out the backrest. Using a jigsaw create a curved top. Actually, with the back rest, you can get creative with how you choose to cut the top portion of the backrest. For this, I just curved the top sides. It's just important that the side and bottom stay straight.

steps.title: Assemble table top
steps.body: WIth the backrest cut out attach Armrest and support legs to the backrest. I recommend attaching the 1x2s together first then attaching it to the backrest.

steps.title: Creating the bench table
steps.body: Mount backrest/table top to toy box base by drilling a hole for the 1/4 in. Hex Cap Screw. Place washers between each board on both sides and secure it with the nut inside the toy box. Then drill a hole for the placement of the eyebolt. This is what will secure the table in place when the backrest is down and in a table position or when it's up in a bench position.
